understanding.goal_understanding: Finish each level by navigating the player to the goal gem tile marked '+', avoiding losing by landing on spike tiles 'u' or 'v', within the allowed attempts and turn limits.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: On each turn I could MOVE_LEFT or MOVE_RIGHT to shift one tile horizontally; after that move, gravity would automatically carry the player in the current gravity direction until landing on a solid/support tile (e.g., o/m/w), hitting a hazard (spikes) which ends the attempt, or reaching the goal '+'. CLICK could be used on specific breakable/clickable tiles (like 'x') at a given [x,y] to remove them and open paths; clicks did not move the player. UNDO would revert the previous action to recover from mistakes. Each attempt had a limited number of turns, with two attempts per level, and failures restart the level.

understanding.goal_understanding: Finish each level by navigating the player to the goal gem tile marked '+', avoiding losing by landing on spike tiles 'u' or 'v', within the allowed attempts and turn limits.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: On each turn I could MOVE_LEFT or MOVE_RIGHT to shift one tile horizontally; after that move, gravity would automatically carry the player in the current gravity direction until landing on a solid/support tile (e.g., o/m/w), hitting a hazard (spikes) which ends the attempt, or reaching the goal '+'. CLICK could be used on specific breakable/clickable tiles (like 'x') at a given [x,y] to remove them and open paths; clicks did not move the player. UNDO would revert the previous action to recover from mistakes. Each attempt had a limited number of turns, with two attempts per level, and failures restart the level.
